High trust in science and experts and high fact-checking behavior support transparency mandates, while low nationalism and cosmopolitan orientation reduce the perceived threat from foreign actors, and low authoritarianism limits support for enforcement.
High nationalism, ethnic nationalism, and dangerous worldview strongly support transparency mandates to counter foreign threats, reinforced by high trust in government.

High fact-checking behavior (95.88) and moderate trust in government (52.42) provide mild support, but very low nationalism (10.58) and high anti-censorship (98.86) limit enthusiasm for foreign-focused mandates.

network_context.edge_meaning: The source position is a visible peer whose output was plausibly exposed to the target position; derived from source user liking, reposting, or quoting target user content in the raw data.

target_network_position.edge_meaning: The source position is a visible peer whose output was plausibly exposed to the target position; derived from source user liking, reposting, or quoting target user content in the raw data.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Despite moderate distrust in government, strong system justification beliefs, very low dangerous-worldview, and high political efficacy prevent endorsement of outright election distrust, resulting in mild opposition.
Very low political trust and system justification, combined with a dangerous worldview, produce strong distrust in elections, though high dutifulness prevents maximal opposition.

Extremely low dangerous-worldview and competitive-jungle worldview, combined with high trust in the judiciary and police and moderate trust in government, produce very strong rejection of election distrust as paranoid and unfounded.
Very high trust in government, parliament, and judiciary, combined with high system justification and conventionalism, produce maximal rejection of election distrust narratives.

Moderate trust in government (52.42) and high system justification (85.24) produce mild disagreement with election distrust, though high anti-elitism (48.1) and low openness (10.72) prevent strong rejection.
